tex:
(a) The diagram shows two rectangles that are mathematically similar.

The small rectangle has dimensions 1.5 cm by 2.5 cm.\\
The large rectangle has dimensions 4.5 cm by 7.5 cm.

(i) (b) Explain why these rectangles are mathematically similar.

(c) How many times bigger is the area of the large rectangle than the area of the small rectangle?

(d) The diagram shows a net of a cube.

The square labelled B is the base.

(i) (e) Write the letter T in the square that is the top of the cube.

(f) On the grid, draw a different net of this cube.

(g) The diagram shows a cuboid.

The cuboid has dimensions 6 cm by 4 cm by 3 cm.

(i) (h) Work out the surface area of this cuboid.

(i) Work out the volume of this cuboid.

(j) Write down the dimensions of a different cuboid that has the same volume as this cuboid.

(a) Each of 196 candidates has a candidate number from 3001 to 3196.
The candidates sit in numerical order in columns and rows, as shown in the diagram.
There are 20 rows.

The diagram shows part of the plan for where the candidates sit.

\begin{tabular}{ccccc}
 & Column 1 & Column 2 & Column 3 & Column 4 \\
Row 20 & 3020 & & & \\
Row 3 & 3003 & 3023 & A & \\
Row 2 & 3002 & 3022 & 3042 & \\
Row 1 & 3001 & 3021 & 3041 & B
\end{tabular}

(i) (b) The diagram shows where candidates A and B sit.

Write down their numbers.

A 

B 

(c) Complete this statement.

Candidate 3135 sits in Column  , Row 

(d) Candidate C sits in Column n, Row 1.

Find an expression, in terms of n, for the number of candidate C.

(e) The geography examination lasts for 1 hour 40 minutes.
Hari is allowed 25\% extra time for his geography examination.

Work out the total time Hari has for this examination.
Give your answer in hours and minutes.

 h  min

(f) The number of examinations that each of the 196 candidates takes is recorded in the table.

\begin{tabular}{ccccc}
Number of examinations & 5 & 6 & 7 & 8 \\
Number of candidates & 27 & 42 & 63 & 64
\end{tabular}

(i) (g) Write down the range.

(h) Find the median.

(i) Calculate the mean.

(j) A candidate is selected at random.

Find the probability that the candidate takes fewer than 8 examinations.

tex:
(a) 25 students go on a water sports trip.
The students each choose their favourite water sport.
These are the results.

\begin{tabular}{ccccc}
Rafting & Fishing & Surfing & Snorkelling & Surfing \\
Snorkelling & Rafting & Kayaking & Rafting & Snorkelling \\
Fishing & Surfing & Surfing & Kayaking & Surfing \\
Fishing & Snorkelling & Surfing & Surfing & Rafting \\
Rafting & Fishing & Snorkelling & Snorkelling & Surfing
\end{tabular}

(i) (b) Complete the frequency table for the results.
You may use the tally column to help you.

\begin{tabular}{|l|c|c|}
\hline
Favourite water sport & Tally & Frequency \\
\hline
Rafting & & \\
\hline
Surfing & & \\
\hline
Fishing & & \\
\hline
Kayaking & & \\
\hline
Snorkelling & & \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

(c) Write down the mode.

(d) Work out the percentage of students whose favourite water sport is snorkelling.

 \%

(e) The table shows the times of the high and low tides.

\begin{tabular}{ccccc}
Day & 1st high tide & 1st low tide & 2nd high tide & 2nd low tide \\
Monday & 00 30 & 06 09 & 13 12 & 18 35 \\
Tuesday & 01 30 & 07 20 & 14 22 & 19 52 \\
Wednesday & 02 43 & 08 36 & 15 37 & 21 06 \\
Thursday & 03 58 & 09 41 & 16 44 & 22 07 \\
Friday & 05 00 & 10 35 & 17 37 & 22 58
\end{tabular}

(i) (f) Write down the time of the 2nd high tide on Wednesday using the 12-hour clock.

(g) Work out the time between the two low tides on Thursday.

 h  min

(h) The surfing activity starts $1\frac{1}{2}$ hours before the high tide on Tuesday afternoon.

Write down the time that the surfing activity starts.

tex:
(a) Some people go fishing and catch four types of fish.
Some information is shown in the table.

\begin{tabular}{ccc}
Type of fish & Number of fish & Pie chart sector angle \\
Cod & 15 & 120° \\
Mackerel & 9 & 72° \\
Herring & & 32° \\
Tuna & &
\end{tabular}

(i) (b) Complete the table.

(c) Complete the pie chart.

(d) Records show that $\frac{3}{14}$ of all mullet caught are underweight.
In one day, 168 mullet are caught.

Work out the expected number of mullet that are underweight.

(a) Anand, Rahul and Samir go from school to the park each day.
One day, Anand cycles and Rahul walks.
The travel graph shows their journeys.

%\begin{InsertScreenShot}
%    
%\end{InsertScreenShot}

The graph shows distance from school (vertical axis) against time (horizontal axis).\\
Anand's journey is shown as a straight line from (15:00, 4km) to (15:20, 0km).\\
Rahul's journey is shown as a straight line from (15:00, 4km) to (15:40, 0km).

(i) (b) Work out the speed that Anand cycles.
Give your answer in kilometres per hour.

 km/h

(c) Find the number of minutes that Anand arrives at the park before Rahul.

 min

(d) Samir cycles at the same speed as Anand.
He arrives at the park at the same time as Rahul.

Find the time that Samir leaves school.

(e) On another day, Anand cycles 2 km to a bench and then walks the rest of the way to the park.
The travel graph shows his journey.

%\begin{InsertScreenShot}
%    
%\end{InsertScreenShot}

The graph shows Anand cycling from school at 15:00, reaching the bench at 2km, then walking to the park at 4km.

(i) (f) Write down the ratio minutes cycling : minutes walking.
Give your answer in its simplest form.

 : 

(g) Rahul leaves school at the same time as Anand.
Rahul walks 2 km to the bench at a constant speed of 6 km/h.
He then cycles the rest of the way to the park at a constant speed of 15 km/h.

(h) Complete the travel graph for Rahul's journey to the park.

(i) Use your travel graph to find the number of minutes that Anand arrives at the bench before Rahul.

 min

(j) Find the greatest distance between Anand and Rahul as they travel to the park.

 km

 km

On the map, construct and shade the region in which the hospital is to be built.

Work out the bearing of Whitestoke from Bartown.

Complete the equation.

Write this information as an equation.

Seven students want to join the school diving club.
Some information about these students is recorded in the table below.

\begin{tabular}{|l|c|c|c|}
\hline
Name & Month and year of birth & Height (metres) & Distance each student can swim (metres) \\
\hline
Arj & November 2004 & 1.62 & 200 \\
\hline
Biva & October 2006 & 1.43 & 500 \\
\hline
Cala & February 2006 & 1.53 & 1500 \\
\hline
Dainy & January 2007 & 1.56 & 1000 \\
\hline
Elu & December 2005 & 1.64 & 600 \\
\hline
Ful & August 2006 & 1.52 & 1000 \\
\hline
Gani & January 2006 & 1.46 & 1000 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

To join the diving club you must be

• at least 12 years old in March 2018\\
and\\
• at least 150 centimetres tall\\
and\\
• able to swim at least 0.5 kilometres.

(a) Write down the names of the students who can join the club.

(b) The students dive off boards of different heights.
The speed, s m/s, that they enter the water from a board of height h metres, can be found using this formula.

s = $\sqrt{19.6h}$

(i) (c) Calculate the value of s when h = 10.

s = 

(d) Make h the subject of the formula.

h =

tex:
(a) A, B, C and D are four equations of straight line graphs.

A \quad $y = -3x + 4$ \quad B \quad $y = 4x - 3$ \quad C \quad $y = 3x - 4$ \quad D \quad $y = -4x - 3$

(i)  Write down the letter of the graph that

(a) passes through the point $(1, -1)$,

(b) has a y-intercept of 4,

(c) has a gradient of $-4$.

(b) The point $(p, -26)$ lies on the line $y = -3x + 4$.

Work out the value of $p$.

(c) Complete the table of values for $y = x^2 - 10x$.

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
$x$ & $-6$ & $-3$ & $0$ & $3$ & $6$ & $9$ & $12$ & $15$ \\
\hline
$y$ & $96$ & & $-21$ & & $-24$ & & $24$ & $75$ \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

(d) On the grid, draw the graph of $y = x^2 - 10x$ for $-6 \leq x \leq 15$.

(e) Write down the co-ordinates of the lowest point of the graph.
texHtml:
(a) A, B, C and D are four equations of straight line graphs. A \quad $y = -3x + 4$ \quad B \quad $y = 4x - 3$ \quad C \quad $y = 3x - 4$ \quad D \quad $y = -4x - 3$ (i) Write down the letter of the graph that (a) passes through the point $(1, -1)$, (b) has a y-intercept of 4, (c) has a gradient of $-4$. (b) The point $(p, -26)$ lies on the line $y = -3x + 4$. Work out the value of $p$. (c) Complete the table of values for $y = x^2 - 10x$.
$x$$-6$$-3$$0$$3$$6$$9$$12$$15$
$y$$96$$-21$$-24$$24$$75$
(d) On the grid, draw the graph of $y = x^2 - 10x$ for $-6 \leq x \leq 15$. (e) Write down the co-ordinates of the lowest point of the graph.
